# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.208.0/http/server.ts";

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

// --- Security Utilities ---

const ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS = new Set(["users", "products", "orders", "posts"]);
const ALLOWED_FILTER_PARAMS = new Set([
  "status", "role", "category", "tag", "region", "type", "active"
]);

const KEY_SEGMENT_PATTERN = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_\-]{1,64}$/;
const MAX_KEY_DEPTH = 5;
const MAX_RESULTS = 100;
const RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS = 60_000;
const RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS = 100;

const rateLimitMap = new Map<string, { count: number; resetAt: number }>();

function sanitizeKeySegment(segment: string): string | null {
  const trimmed = segment.trim();
  if (!KEY_SEGMENT_PATTERN.test(trimmed)) {
    return null;
  }
  return trimmed;
}

function validateCollection(collection: string): boolean {
  return ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS.has(collection);
}

function validateFilterParam(param: string): boolean {
  return ALLOWED_FILTER_PARAMS.has(param);
}

function buildKvKey(parts: string[]): Deno.KvKey | null {
  if (parts.length === 0 || parts.length > MAX_KEY_DEPTH) {
    return null;
  }

  const sanitized: string[] = [];
  for (const part of parts) {
    const clean = sanitizeKeySegment(part);
    if (clean === null) {
      return null;
    }
    sanitized.push(clean);
  }

  return sanitized as unknown as Deno.KvKey;
}

function checkRateLimit(clientIp: string): boolean {
  const now = Date.now();
  const entry = rateLimitMap.get(clientIp);

  if (!entry || now > entry.resetAt) {
    rateLimitMap.set(clientIp, { count: 1, resetAt: now + RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS });
    return true;
  }

  entry.count++;
  return entry.count <= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS;
}

function getClientIp(request: Request): string {
  return request.headers.get("x-forwarded-for")?.split(",")[0]?.trim() ||
    request.headers.get("cf-connecting-ip") ||
    "unknown";
}

function jsonResponse(data: unknown, status = 200, headers: Record<string, string> = {}): Response {
  return new Response(JSON.stringify(data), {
    status,
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
      "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
      "X-Frame-Options": "DENY",
      "Cache-Control": "no-store",
      ...headers,
    },
  });
}

function errorResponse(message: string, status: number): Response {
  return jsonResponse({ error: message }, status);
}

function sanitizeValueForStorage(value: unknown): unknown {
  if (typeof value === "string") {
    if (value.length > 10_000) {
      throw new Error("Value too large");
    }
    return value;
  }
  if (typeof value === "number" || typeof value === "boolean") {
    return value;
  }
  if (value === null) {
    return null;
  }
  if (Array.isArray(value)) {
    return value.map(sanitizeValueForStorage);
  }
  if (typeof value === "object") {
    const sanitized: Record<string, unknown> = {};
    const entries = Object.entries(value as Record<string, unknown>);
    if (entries.length > 100) {
      throw new Error("Too many fields");
    }
    for (const [k, v] of entries) {
      const cleanKey = sanitizeKeySegment(k);
      if (cleanKey === null) {
        throw new Error(`Invalid field name: ${k}`);
      }
      sanitized[cleanKey] = sanitizeValueForStorage(v);
    }
    return sanitized;
  }
  throw new Error("Unsupported value type");
}

// --- Route Handlers ---

async function handleGetItem(collection: string, id: string): Promise<Response> {
  if (!validateCollection(collection)) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid collection", 400);
  }

  const key = buildKvKey([collection, id]);
  if (!key) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid key parameters", 400);
  }

  const result = await kv.get(key);
  if (!result.value) {
    return errorResponse("Not found", 404);
  }

  return jsonResponse({ data: result.value, versionstamp: result.versionstamp });
}

async function handleGetItemWithFilter(
  collection: string,
  id: string,
  filterParam: string,
  filterValue: string
): Promise<Response> {
  if (!validateCollection(collection)) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid collection", 400);
  }
  if (!validateFilterParam(filterParam)) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid filter parameter", 400);
  }

  const key = buildKvKey([collection, id, filterParam, filterValue]);
  if (!key) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid key parameters", 400);
  }

  const result = await kv.get(key);
  if (!result.value) {
    return errorResponse("Not found", 404);
  }

  return jsonResponse({ data: result.value, versionstamp: result.versionstamp });
}

async function handleSearch(collection: string, searchParams: URLSearchParams): Promise<Response> {
  if (!validateCollection(collection)) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid collection", 400);
  }

  const limitParam = searchParams.get("limit");
  const limit = limitParam ? Math.min(parseInt(limitParam, 10) || 20, MAX_RESULTS) : 20;

  const filters: Record<string, string> = {};
  for (const [param, value] of searchParams.entries()) {
    if (param === "limit" || param === "cursor") continue;
    if (validateFilterParam(param)) {
      const cleanValue = sanitizeKeySegment(value);
      if (cleanValue) {
        filters[param] = cleanValue;
      }
    }
  }

  const filterKeys = Object.keys(filters);

  // If we have exactly one filter, use a prefix scan on the indexed key
  if (filterKeys.length === 1) {
    const filterParam = filterKeys[0];
    const filterValue = filters[filterParam];
    const prefix = buildKvKey([collection, "by_" + filterParam, filterValue]);
    if (!prefix) {
      return errorResponse("Invalid search parameters", 400);
    }

    const results: unknown[] = [];
    const iter = kv.list({ prefix }, { limit });
    for await (const entry of iter) {
      results.push({ key: entry.key, value: entry.value });
    }

    return jsonResponse({ data: results, count: results.length, filters });
  }

  // No specific filter or multiple filters: list all in collection and filter in memory
  const prefix = buildKvKey([collection]);
  if (!prefix) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid collection", 400);
  }

  const results: unknown[] = [];
  const iter = kv.list({ prefix }, { limit: limit * 10 }); // over-fetch for filtering

  for await (const entry of iter) {
    if (results.length >= limit) break;

    const value = entry.value as Record<string, unknown> | null;
    if (!value || typeof value !== "object") continue;

    // Apply all filters
    let matches = true;
    for (const [param, filterVal] of Object.entries(filters)) {
      if (String(value[param]) !== filterVal) {
        matches = false;
        break;
      }
    }

    if (matches) {
      results.push({ key: entry.key, value });
    }
  }

  return jsonResponse({ data: results, count: results.length, filters });
}

async function handleCreateItem(collection: string, body: unknown): Promise<Response> {
  if (!validateCollection(collection)) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid collection", 400);
  }

  if (!body || typeof body !== "object" || Array.isArray(body)) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid request body", 400);
  }

  const record = body as Record<string, unknown>;
  const id = record.id ? String(record.id) : crypto.randomUUID();
  const cleanId = sanitizeKeySegment(id);
  if (!cleanId) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid id", 400);
  }

  let sanitizedData: unknown;
  try {
    sanitizedData = sanitizeValueForStorage({ ...record, id: cleanId });
  } catch (e) {
    return errorResponse(`Validation error: ${(e as Error).message}`, 400);
  }

  const primaryKey = buildKvKey([collection, cleanId]);
  if (!primaryKey) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid key", 400);
  }

  // Build atomic operation with primary key and secondary indexes
  let atomic = kv.atomic().set(primaryKey, sanitizedData);

  // Create secondary index entries for known filter params
  for (const param of ALLOWED_FILTER_PARAMS) {
    if (param in record) {
      const filterValue = sanitizeKeySegment(String(record[param]));
      if (filterValue) {
        const indexKey = buildKvKey([collection, "by_" + param, filterValue, cleanId]);
        if (indexKey) {
          atomic = atomic.set(indexKey, sanitizedData);
        }
      }
    }
  }

  const result = await atomic.commit();
  if (!result.ok) {
    return errorResponse("Failed to create item", 500);
  }

  return jsonResponse({ data: sanitizedData, id: cleanId }, 201);
}

async function handleUpdateItem(collection: string, id: string, body: unknown): Promise<Response> {
  if (!validateCollection(collection)) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid collection", 400);
  }

  const cleanId = sanitizeKeySegment(id);
  if (!cleanId) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid id", 400);
  }

  if (!body || typeof body !== "object" || Array.isArray(body)) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid request body", 400);
  }

  let sanitizedData: unknown;
  try {
    sanitizedData = sanitizeValueForStorage({ ...(body as Record<string, unknown>), id: cleanId });
  } catch (e) {
    return errorResponse(`Validation error: ${(e as Error).message}`, 400);
  }

  const primaryKey = buildKvKey([collection, cleanId]);
  if (!primaryKey) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid key", 400);
  }

  // Get existing record to clean up old indexes
  const existing = await kv.get(primaryKey);
  if (!existing.value) {
    return errorResponse("Not found", 404);
  }

  const existingRecord = existing.value as Record<string, unknown>;
  const newRecord = sanitizedData as Record<string, unknown>;

  let atomic = kv.atomic()
    .check(existing) // optimistic concurrency
    .set(primaryKey, sanitizedData);

  // Update secondary indexes
  for (const param of ALLOWED_FILTER_PARAMS) {
    // Remove old index entry
    if (param in existingRecord) {
      const oldValue = sanitizeKeySegment(String(existingRecord[param]));
      if (oldValue) {
        const oldIndexKey = buildKvKey([collection, "by_" + param, oldValue, cleanId]);
        if (oldIndexKey) {
          atomic = atomic.delete(oldIndexKey);
        }
      }
    }
    // Add new index entry
    if (param in newRecord) {
      const newValue = sanitizeKeySegment(String(newRecord[param]));
      if (newValue) {
        const newIndexKey = buildKvKey([collection, "by_" + param, newValue, cleanId]);
        if (newIndexKey) {
          atomic = atomic.set(newIndexKey, sanitizedData);
        }
      }
    }
  }

  const result = await atomic.commit();
  if (!result.ok) {
    return errorResponse("Conflict: item was modified by another request", 409);
  }

  return jsonResponse({ data: sanitizedData });
}

async function handleDeleteItem(collection: string, id: string): Promise<Response> {
  if (!validateCollection(collection)) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid collection", 400);
  }

  const cleanId = sanitizeKeySegment(id);
  if (!cleanId) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid id", 400);
  }

  const primaryKey = buildKvKey([collection, cleanId]);
  if (!primaryKey) {
    return errorResponse("Invalid key", 400);
  }

  // Get existing to clean up indexes
  const existing = await kv.get(primaryKey);
  if (!existing.value) {
    return errorResponse("Not found", 404);
  }

  const existingRecord = existing.value as Record<string, unknown>;
  let atomic = kv.atomic().check(existing).delete(primaryKey);

  for (const param of ALLOWED_FILTER_PARAMS) {
    if (param in existingRecord) {
      const filterValue = sanitizeKeySegment(String(existingRecord[param]));
      if (filterValue) {
        const indexKey = buildKvKey([collection, "by_" + param, filterValue, cleanId]);
        if (indexKey) {
          atomic = atomic.delete(indexKey);
        }
      }
    }
  }

  const result = await atomic.commit();
  if (!result.ok) {
    return errorResponse("Conflict during deletion", 409);
  }

  return jsonResponse({ deleted: true, id: cleanId });
}

// --- Main Router ---

async function handler(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const clientIp = getClientIp(request);

  if (!checkRateLimit(clientIp)) {
    return errorResponse("Rate limit exceeded", 429);
  }

  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const pathSegments = url.pathname.split("/").filter(Boolean);

  // CORS preflight
  if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
    return new Response(null, {
      status: 204,
      headers: {
        "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
        "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS",
        "Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "Content-Type, Authorization",
        "Access-Control-Max-Age": "86400",
      },
    });
  }

  // Health check
  if (pathSegments.length === 0 || (pathSegments.length === 1 && pathSegments[0] === "health")) {
    return jsonResponse({ status: "ok", collections: [...ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS] });
  }

  // Routes: /api/{collection}, /api/{collection}/{id}, /api/{collection}/search
  if (pathSegments[0] !== "api" || pathSegments.length < 2) {
    return errorResponse("Not found", 404);
  }

  const collection = pathSegments[1];

  try {
    // GET /api/{collection}/search?param=value&limit=20
    if (pathSegments.length === 3 && pathSegments[2] === "search" && request.method === "GET") {
      return await handleSearch(collection, url.searchParams);
    }

    // GET /api/{collection}/{id}?filterParam=filterValue (optional filter)
    if (pathSegments.length === 3 && request.method === "GET") {
      const id = pathSegments[2];

      // Check if there's a filter query param for sub-key lookup
      const filterEntries = [...url.searchParams.entries()].filter(
        ([k]) => validateFilterParam(k)
      );

      if (filterEntries.length === 1) {
        const [filterParam, filterValue] = filterEntries[0];
        return await handleGetItemWithFilter(collection, id, filterParam, filterValue);
      }

      return await handleGetItem(collection, id);
    }

    // POST /api/{collection} - create
    if (pathSegments.length === 2 && request.method === "POST") {
      const contentType = request.headers.get("content-type");
      if (!contentType?.includes("application/json")) {
        return errorResponse("Content-Type must be application/json", 415);
      }

      const bodyText = await request.text();
      if (bodyText.length > 1_000_000) {
        return errorResponse("Request body too large", 413);
      }

      let body: unknown;
      try {
        body = JSON.parse(bodyText);
      } catch {
        return errorResponse("Invalid JSON", 400);
      }

      return await handleCreateItem(collection, body);
    }

    // PUT /api/{collection}/{id} - update
    if (pathSegments.length === 3 && request.method === "PUT") {
      const contentType = request.headers.get("content-type");
      if (!contentType?.includes("application/json")) {
        return errorResponse("Content-Type must be application/json", 415);
      }

      const bodyText = await request.text();
      if (bodyText.length > 1_000_000) {
        return errorResponse("Request body too large", 413);
      }

      let body: unknown;
      try {
        body = JSON.parse(bodyText);
      } catch {
        return errorResponse("Invalid JSON", 400);
      }

      return await handleUpdateItem(collection, pathSegments[2], body);
    }

    // DELETE /api/{collection}/{id}
    if (pathSegments.length === 3 && request.method === "DELETE") {
      return await handleDeleteItem(collection, pathSegments[2]);
    }

    return errorResponse("Not found", 404);
  } catch (e) {
    console.error("Unhandled error:", e);
    return errorResponse("Internal server error", 500);
  }
}

serve(handler, { port: 8000 });
